videos.title: Pelotonia 2012 - Team Huntington | Huntington Bank
videos.description:
At Huntington, we dream of an end to cancer. That's why we participate in Pelotonia every year. Pelotonia is an annual Columbus, Ohio-based grassroots bike ride that raises money for cancer research. Watch to learn more about Team Huntington's experience at Pelotonia and what it means to us. Learn how you can help end cancer through Pelotonia at: https://www.huntington.com/endcancer?cmpid=seo:pelotonia:pelotonia_2012:youtube&sc_camp=6EE85565A74A4E8FCDD6D80F6BA3EAE7&spacer=0

Donate to Pelotonia: https://pelotonia.org/donate?recipient=a0s3t00000BKX8sAAH&recipientType=Peloton
Follow us on Facebook:  https://www.facebook.com/HuntingtonBank
Follow us on Instagram:  https://www.instagram.com/huntingtonbank
Follow us on LinkedIn:  https://www.linkedin.com/company/huntington-national-bank
Follow us on Twitter:  https://twitter.com/Huntington_Bank

videos.title: A look back at Pelotonia Gravel Day 2025 | OSUCCC – James
videos.channel: Ohio State University Comprehensive Cancer Center-James Cancer Hospital & Solove Research Institute
videos.description:
The Pelotonia community hit Ohio's rocky roads to support Ohio State's cancer research during Gravel Day 2025. 

The third annual event brought riders — and for the first time, walkers and runners — together for rugged biking and hiking adventures, live music, food and more to raise money for cancer research at the OSUCCC – James.

More than 530 participants and 200 volunteers took part in the  October 4 event, gathering outside Nelsonville, Ohio, in Athens County to ride 11, 25 or 48 miles on rocky terrain through wooded paths. Over 350 people opted to try the trail running and hiking course that was introduced this year. Thanks to everyone who made the event a huge success! 

Click to learn how you can support Pelotonia's mission of ending cancer through groundbreaking research at the OSUCCC – James: https://pelotonia.org

Subscribe to The James on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@UCXGasnfbCpWYVW2RLTDPn4Q

Learn more about cancer care and research at The Ohio State University: 
Home: https://cancer.osu.edu
Blog: https://cancer.osu.edu/blog
News: https://cancer.osu.edu/news 

Follow The James on social media:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/OSUCCCJames
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/osuccc_james/
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ohio-state-university-comprehensive-cancer-center-james-cancer-hospital-&-solove-research-institute
X: https://x.com/OSUCCC_James
tags: Ohio State University Comprehensive Cancer Center
The James Cancer Hospital and Solove Research Institute
Ohio State University James Cancer Hospital

videos.description: A timelapse video of my ride from Columbus to Athens, OH for Pelotonia 2011.  Recorded with GoPro HD.

videos.title: How personal cancer journey inspires Pelotonia CEO | OSUCCC – James
videos.channel: Ohio State University Comprehensive Cancer Center-James Cancer Hospital & Solove Research Institute
videos.description:
Cancer survivor Joe Apgar's passion for Pelotonia began long before he became CEO. 

“I’ll never forget how I felt in the moment someone told me I had cancer,” says Apgar, who became CEO of the fundraising cycling event organization in 2024. “You feel completely lost and by yourself and you don’t have answers to the questions running around through your head.” 

Apgar, who was diagnosed with testicular cancer while a college student in Pennsylvania, first participated in Pelotonia after moving to Columbus in 2011 and helping to create a team sponsored by his company.

“I remember standing at my first Pelotonia Opening Ceremony, and how uplifted and excited and supported I felt,” he says. “That’s when I could flip the switch on and feel empowered by my own story and experience.”

The inspiration only grew in Apgar after that 2011 event, eventually leading him to join the Pelotonia leadership team under CEO Doug Ulman. After years of partnership, they decided to change their roles to better position the organization to grow in the future.

“Doug and I have spent a lot of time working together over the last five or six years, and really working through where our different skill sets lie,” says Apgar about the joint decision to assume the CEO role while Ulman took on a position as vice chair of the board. “We've got some really big goals, and I think this is the next step towards achieving those.”

Since its inception in 2008, Pelotonia has raised more than $283 million for cancer research at The Ohio State University Comprehensive Cancer Center – James Cancer Hospital and Solove Research Institute. Among the initiatives funded, at least in part, by the Pelotonia community are Ohio State’s Pelotonia Research Center and the Pelotonia Institute for Immuno-Oncology.

That funding has been made possible by countless riders, donors and volunteers, as well as Apgar and his Pelotonia colleagues, who have worked together to expand from the initial weekend ride to a series of events and fundraisers, including a new Gravel Day road ride that began in 2023.

“I think [the Pelotonia founders] hoped that some of this sense of community would happen, but I don’t think anyone could have dreamed it would happen at the scale it has,” Apgar says.

Learn more about Pelotonia, and register for the 2024 rides: https://pelotonia.org

Learn more about cancer care and research at The Ohio State University: 
Home: https://cancer.osu.edu
Blog: https://cancer.osu.edu/blog
News: https://cancer.osu.edu/news

Follow The James on social media:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/OSUCCCJames
X (formerly Twitter): https://twitter.com/OSUCCC_James
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/osuccc_james/
tags: Ohio State University Comprehensive Cancer Center
The James Cancer Hospital and Solove Research Institute
Ohio State University James Cancer Hospital

videos.title: Last Training Ride Before Pelotonia (In the Rain!)
videos.description: In her last ride prior to Pelotonia, a 100 mile ride to end cancer, Julie documents her thoughts about the big day.  Complete with a rainstorm in the background, Julie talks about 'learning to dance in the rain' instead of waiting for the rain to end.
videos.transcript:
hey guys I'm on my last training ride
before pelonia 2012 and I don't know if
you can hear but it is raining but you
know patonia is about ending cancer
forever it's a bike ride that pledges to
raise money that all goes towards the
research that will end cancer forever
and cancer doesn't go away in the rain
so neither do we
so um so I hope that you can forgive the
raindrops as I'm speaking and telling
you a little bit about my last ride so
as I was riding tonight I thought about
patonia and I'd like to get a little bit
um you know emotional and spiritual
before I do anything big like this and I
was thinking about how privileged I am
to be able to be a part of such an
amazing event this is my third year
riding the 100 mile course and I'm
overwhelmed with the amount of support
I've received from my friends from my
family from strangers people giving
money and support and kind words that
don't even know me but you know that's
the cool thing about our community we
all know that cancer doesn't
discriminate cancer doesn't pick healthy
people or sick people or people of one
way or another cancer picks us all you
know and so that's why we've got to do
this that's why we've got to band
together as a group and do things like
pelonia where we pledge that each one of
us has a role to play and maybe I'm not
the researcher that's going to find the
Cure but I can be somebody that helps to
raise awareness and help to ask people
to give money so that uh you know that
researcher can be funded so that they
eventually can find their
cure and so I just want to say thank you
to pelonia to the staff to Tom Lennox
the Visionary behind this event it is
always so well-run it is always so
amazing and you know when we cross that
Finish Line when we cross that finish
line on Saturday it's not going to be
about each one of us Crossing that line
sure it's a big deal to ride 100 miles
especially because I'm not actually much
of a cyclist this is a new sport for me
that I've been learning over the past
few years and uh so it will be a
personal accomplishment but when we
cross that line that's the last thing
we'll be thinking about what we're going
to be thinking about is the fact that we
came together and we were a part of
something that helped to crush cancer
and that is what's most important to
each and every single one of us and so I
want to say thank you to my friends my
family to The Strangers who who have
supported me to all of you writers out
there to all of you who are going to get
up and cheer us on and all of you who
have in one way or another helped us
encouraged us and pushed us to be our
best on this day it is an honor to be a
part of it and I really felt like I
wanted to give that message out tonight
prior to writing now I look forward to
sharing another video blog with you once
I'm done of all the Amazing Adventures I
had along the way cuz I know it's going
to be a powerful powerful day but I want
to say thank you and um you know like I
said cancer doesn't hide in the rain so
we won't cancer doesn't hide in the snow
cancer doesn't hide in in the sunshine
cancer is everywhere with us all the
time but we're going to put an end to
that hey guys well little addendum here
a little additional vlogging as it's
pouring rain and I'm cycling took a
break at an intersection to let you know
you know the the rain didn't let up in
fact it's gotten a bit worse and so I'm
not going to stay long with my phone out
in the rain but what I do want to tell
you is a quote that's so relative to
this weekend and that is instead of
waiting for the rain to end it's about
learning to dance in the rain so no
matter what your predicament no matter
what you're in it's about learning just
to celebrate the space you're in and do
all the good you can in all the ways you
can for all the people you can for as
long as you ever can I think that's a
kind of a partial from John
Wesley but you know no matter what rain
sleep hail whatever it's not going to
keep us back from Crossing that finish
line on Saturday because we got
something way more
important finally made it home I don't
know if you can tell this rain I don't
know if you can see it but it's
definitely a big downpour my Fe are
stopping wet but you know we don't stop
we don't stop because cancer doesn't
stop and we're not stopping until we
find a carot
